other_observers: Andrew Spencer, Paul Oppler; I am original finder and identifier. A Spencer accompanied me.
specific_location: Northwest shore of Boyd Lake, at Boyd Lake State Park
description: A gull observed at the edge of a lake, appearing intermediate in size between accompanying Ring-billed Gulls and Herring Gulls, with all white feathers on head and underparts. Upperparts pale gray (palere than accompanying gulls) on mantle with broad white tertial crescent and small white scapular crescent. Primaries mostly snow white, but each primary with a subterminal patch of pale gray. Tail white. Legs pink. Iris yellow. Bill pale yellow, with a small red spot at gonys. Bill smaller than that of Herring Gull, with weak gonydeal angle.Behaviors: Walking in shallow water, standing, sleeping, preening.Call: not heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: a spencer, a spencer, a spencer, a spencer, a spencer
